hero.tagline: From theory to industry disruption — understand what smart contracts can and cannot do for your business.

section1.title: Introduction to Smart Contracts
section1.description: What smart contracts are, the Nick Szabo vending machine model, and the historical evolution from 1994 to today.

section2.title: How Smart Contracts Work
section2.description: Workflow, core components, execution environment, the Web3 landscape, dApps, gas economics, and why you should build with smart contracts.

section3.title: Industries & Case Studies
section3.description: Five industry verticals (DeFi, Gaming/NFTs, RWA, Supply Chain, Digital Cert) plus eight flagship cases including Walmart, Santander, Estonia, The DAO, and ASX.

section4.title: Critical Thinking
section4.description: Decision framework: when to use a smart contract — and when not to. Plus the 5-pillar adoption model for serious business deployment.

section5.title: Limitations & Risks
section5.description: Oracle problem, blockchain trilemma, security risks (reentrancy, flash loans, MEV), cost realities, and regulatory uncertainty.

section6.title: Build & Integration
section6.description: ERC standards, EIP process, frameworks (Hardhat / Foundry), 17-platform comparison, integration patterns, and AI-driven development.

section7.title: Team Project
section7.description: Design a smart contract business application, map stakeholders, identify risks, and present your case to the class.

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.description: Smart contracts are not magic — they are code. Their power and their risk come from the same source.
